purpose: Phase implementation-group verdicts generated after grouped implementation batches. Use this as a bounded group-repair queue before the normal phase coverage gate.

repairPolicy.blockedMeans: explicit blocker/fallback decision exists; do not loop on this group before phase gate
repairPolicy.completeMeans: linked source/platform authority rows have enough behavior, parity, endpoint, and evidence data

groups.title: Source behavior: presentation/login
reasons: app-src-main-java-com-example-foodike-presentation-login-1ed35246b3-002-navigation-surface-menu-drawer-toolbar-o: implemented with behavior, parity, endpoint, and verification evidence
app-src-main-java-com-example-foodike-presentation-login-78d5c742f4-007-observable-state-viewmodel-flow-livedata: implemented with behavior, parity, endpoint, and verification evidence
app-src-main-java-com-example-foodike-presentation-login-2c65d08c3b-005-screen-lifecycle-entry-point-initializat: implemented with behavior, parity, endpoint, and verification evidence
app-src-main-java-com-example-foodike-presentation-login-34c5f4ba3a-006-settings-preferences-option-toggles-or-c: implemented with behavior, parity, endpoint, and verification evidence
requiredBehaviors: Source review of LoginScreen.kt found only static social-login icons rendered as tappable affordances; the screen does not open a bitmap viewer, gallery preview, zoom surface, or EXIF/media flow.

LoginScreen triggers a successful transition to `Screen.Home.route` from the login button callback, popping the start destination so onboarding/login are cleared from the stack instead of remaining on back navigation.
Source review of LoginScreen.kt found no notification publishing, widget update, RemoteViews, or home-screen integration behavior; the file only renders the login form and placeholder actions.

LoginScreen observes Compose state from `viewModel.email` and `viewModel.password`, and it collects `eventFlow` in `LaunchedEffect` so text changes and failed-login snackbar events recompose immediately while the screen stays active.

Source review of LoginScreen.kt found no runtime permission request, permission callback, or protected platform API access; the login flow uses only local text entry, toasts, snackbar messaging, and persisted login-state mutation through the view model.

LoginScreen performs screen-entry setup by creating scaffold state, wiring a `LaunchedEffect(key1 = true)` collector for one-shot UI events, and applying status/navigation bar colors while the login screen is active.

Successful login triggers persisted session-state mutation through `LoginViewModel` and `LoginRepository.toggleLoginState()`, so the screen changes a stored login preference rather than only local UI state.
acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.
Build pass alone is not per-feature verification.
Referenced target tests are not strong evidence until a latest test gate PASS or explicit harness blocker is recorded.
riskHints: critical/high behavior cannot close on build pass alone

groups.title: Source behavior: presentation/onboarding
reasons: app-src-main-java-com-example-foodike-presentation-onboa-d591d5d894-003-data-loading-query-search-filter-or-sort: implemented with behavior, parity, endpoint, and verification evidence
app-src-main-java-com-example-foodike-presentation-onboa-eed8afd34d-001-navigation-surface-menu-drawer-toolbar-o: implemented with behavior, parity, endpoint, and verification evidence
app-src-main-java-com-example-foodike-presentation-onboa-579cddd1b6-004-observable-state-viewmodel-flow-livedata: implemented with behavior, parity, endpoint, and verification evidence
app-src-main-java-com-example-foodike-presentation-onboa-fcaf31ccc3-003-screen-lifecycle-entry-point-initializat: implemented with behavior, parity, endpoint, and verification evidence
requiredBehaviors: OnboardingPage renders a static drawable from `item.image` as the top-half hero illustration for each onboarding slide, but it does not expose image preview, zoom, EXIF inspection, gallery browsing, or bitmap-manipulation behavior.
OnboardingPage only lays out static image and text content for each onboarding slide; it does not request runtime permissions, handle permission callbacks, or access protected platform APIs.

`OnBoardingItem.get()` loads the full onboarding data set by returning a fixed ordered list of three slide definitions. The method is the source of onboarding page content and preserves the slide order used by the pager.

`OnBoardingItem.get()` returns three resource IDs for onboarding slide artwork, titles, and descriptions. It supplies static drawable references only and does not implement image preview, zoom, or metadata behavior.
`OnBoardingItem.get()` is a pure in-memory list factory for onboarding slide resources. It does not request permissions or reach protected APIs.
OnBoarding owns the pager navigation surface: tapping the trailing action advances to the next page until the last slide, then navigates to `Screen.LoginScreen.route`.
OnBoarding observes pager state through `pagerState.currentPage` so indicator selection and next/final action behavior update immediately as the user swipes or taps next.
OnBoarding coordinates pager state, window color styling, and login navigation only; it does not request permissions, process permission results, or use protected APIs.
OnBoarding performs route-entry setup by creating coroutine and pager state, loading slide items, and applying onboarding-specific status/navigation bar colors while the onboarding screen is active.
BottomSection renders only onboarding indicators and a trailing next button. It does not request permissions or access protected APIs.
Indicators and Indicator render animated onboarding progress pills only. They do not request permissions or touch protected APIs.
acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.
Build pass alone is not per-feature verification.
Referenced target tests are not strong evidence until a latest test gate PASS or explicit harness blocker is recorded.
riskHints: critical/high behavior cannot close on build pass alone

groups.title: Source behavior: presentation/profile
reasons: app-src-main-java-com-example-foodike-presentation-profi-c34aae0a57-002-navigation-surface-menu-drawer-toolbar-o: implemented with behavior, parity, endpoint, and verification evidence
app-src-main-java-com-example-foodike-presentation-profi-e492afed0c-004-observable-state-viewmodel-flow-livedata: implemented with behavior, parity, endpoint, and verification evidence
app-src-main-java-com-example-foodike-presentation-profi-1b04c65221-003-observable-state-viewmodel-flow-livedata: implemented with behavior, parity, endpoint, and verification evidence
requiredBehaviors: Profile.kt renders a static circular avatar from `R.drawable.ic_profile` plus an inert edit affordance, but it does not open a bitmap viewer, gallery preview, zoom surface, or EXIF/media metadata flow.

Profile.kt owns the profile navigation surface: the top-left back icon calls `navController.navigateUp()`, the logout button dispatches `ProfileEvent.PerformLogout`, and the logout callback navigates to onboarding while clearing the stack back to the graph...

Profile.kt keeps most profile content static, but it propagates UI actions through `ProfileViewModel.onEvent(ProfileEvent.PerformLogout)` and callback lambdas so logout navigation happens only after the view-model layer processes the event.

Profile.kt renders static profile content, placeholder card actions, and logout navigation only; it does not request runtime permissions, handle permission callbacks, or access protected platform APIs.

ProfileViewModel.onEvent` handles `ProfileEvent.PerformLogout` by launching a coroutine, toggling persisted login state through `LoginRepository`, and only then invoking the supplied callback so navigation happens after session mutation.

ProfileViewModel.kt contains only logout-event handling through `repository.toggleLoginState()` and a completion callback; it does not request permissions, process permission results, or touch protected APIs.
ProfileViewModel.kt has no init block, resume/pause refresh path, teardown override, or other screen lifecycle ownership; the file only reacts to a logout event when invoked by the UI.
acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.
Build pass alone is not per-feature verification.
riskHints: critical/high behavior cannot close on build pass alone
